title: Occult Downtime Events
description: Events, discoveries, and obligations that arrive during the crew's downtime — the things that happen when they're not actively on a job.

entries.result: A letter arrives for a crew member from someone they haven't heard from in years. The return address is a city the sender has never been to.
A contact surfaces with information they're offering at no charge. Free information from this source has, historically, not been free.
A crew member has begun experiencing something minor but consistent — a sound, a smell, a recurring dream — that started the night after the last job.
An item from a previous job surfaces in a public context — on display, in the news, in a market — in ways that suggest it's been circulating since.
A faction the crew has worked adjacent to extends an invitation to a private event. The nature of the event isn't specified.
A crew member's name appears in a document that is circulating in their area of the occult underground.
Someone who the crew believed was not aware of their existence sends word that they would like to meet.
A resource the crew depends on is temporarily unavailable. The reason given is innocuous. The timing is suspicious.
A former associate resents something the crew did, correctly. They are choosing not to act on this resentment for now.
An opportunity to acquire something useful surfaces through a third party. The opportunity has a short window. The provenance is unclear.
A location the crew uses is under increased general surveillance unrelated to their activities. They need to adjust their habits.
A member of the crew receives a visit from a relative or old friend who is not aware of what they do.
A practitioner the crew knows has taken a job that puts them on a collision course with the crew's interests.
An old obligation that a crew member incurred before the group formed is being called in. The obligation is theirs personally.
The city's occult underground shifts alignment — a faction falls, a new one rises, an alliance forms — in ways that change the landscape the crew operates in.
Credible rumors reach the crew that someone has been asking about them. The description of the asker doesn't match anyone they know.
A new piece of information comes to light about a previous job that, if accurate, means the crew accomplished something different from what they were hired to do.
A crew member has attracted the notice of an entity — not hostile, just interested — that has been appearing at the edges of their routine.
A job offer arrives that conflicts directly with an existing relationship or obligation. The conflict appears calculated.
Nothing visible happens during downtime. Three separate contacts independently mention that the city has gone quiet in a way that suggests preparation.
A client from a previous job requests a meeting to discuss the scope of what was retrieved — specifically, whether the crew is aware of everything that was retrieved.
A piece of equipment the crew uses has developed a property it didn't previously have. Small. Consistent. Unexplained.
A crew member's reputation has been improved in a specific circle of the underground in ways they didn't earn during the last job. Someone has credited them with something.
A crew member's reputation has been damaged in a specific circle of the underground by a rumor that is precisely inaccurate in a way that suggests intent.
A neutral party has offered to hold an item on the crew's behalf that they have been unable to safely store.
An investigator has closed a case that was adjacent to one of the crew's previous operations. Their conclusion is wrong but safe. The crew can let it stand or correct it.
A significant entity is leaving the city. This changes the landscape in ways that depend on what the entity was doing here.
A significant entity is arriving in the city. The word is out in occult channels before it arrives.
A patron offers the crew a retainer in exchange for exclusivity. The terms are favorable. The cost is independence.
Something the crew has been holding — an object, a secret, an obligation — has become more valuable since they acquired it, in a market they didn't know existed.
